items.original_title: App should have confirmation dialog before sign out
items.original_body:
# Actual Result
Currently, tapping to "sign out" button, the user will be logged out immediately
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/69950655/105316656-c417b280-5bf3-11eb-9e02-0cea71be4cfc.mp4


# Expected Result
The app should have a confirmation dialog asking the user to be sure when signing out

# Environment
Android app
items.mutated_title: App should have confirmation dialog before sign out
items.mutated_body:
"# Actual Result
Currently, tapping the 'sign out' button, the user will be logged out immediately
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/69950655/105316656-c417b280-5bf3-11eb-9e02-0cea71be4cfc.mp4

# Expected Result
The app should have a confirmation dialog asking the user for confirmation before signing out

# Environment
Android app"

items.original_body:
Hello,

I'm using C++ TraCIAPI client library to retrieve simulation information from SUMO. I follow the instruction I check the person value retrieval website (https://sumo.dlr.de/docs/TraCI/Person_Value_Retrieval.html). There are several functions I cannot find in the C++ TraCI: _getWidth, getMinGap, getTaxiReservation_ . I know python got these method, but I need to use C++ run my code. Will these missing functions be added to the C++ TraCIAPI?
items.mutated_title: Missing traCI functions in C++ cf7
items.mutated_body:
Hello,

I'm using C++ TraCIAPI client library to retrieve simulation information from SUMO. I follow the instruction I check the person value retrieval website (https://sumo.dlr.de/docs/TraCI/Person_Value_Retrieval.html). There are several functions I cannot find in the C++ TraCI: _getWidth, getMinGap, getTaxiReservation_ . I know python got these method, but I need to use C++ run my code. Will these missing functions be added to the C++ TraCIAPI?

items.original_title: vn medical needs better compat with the entire revive system
items.original_body:
**Describe the bug**
on the VN branch, medical is broken for AI in askHelp, checking only for vanilla medical items when determining if an AI is capable of helping other units, theres likely more bugs like this hidden in the system, although the entire system majorly needs a rework, this is critical for low player counts where the players rely on AI to help them keep going against the numbers thrown at them, where larger groups would count on there superior numbers of players to help each other.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the bug (If you can't reproduce it, just give details of what happened beforehand. Anything you think could be relevant): get knocked down with medical AI nearby, they will not be counted, and the system will claim that no units capable of helping you are nearby.


**Expected behaviour**
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Environment (please complete the following information):**
Version: 
Map (specify if Altis Blufor): 

* [ ] LAN Hosted
* [ ] Private dedicated server
* [ ] Private dedicated server with Headless client(s)
* [ ] Community Server 1
* [ ] Community Server 2
* [ ] Community Test Server
* [ ] Community Test Server 2

Modset:
 
Time the bug occurred (Please leave in GMT+0):
If **Not** on a community server, please leave the time zone of your client for LAN, or the server for dedicated: 

**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.



If you were LAN hosting, please attach your client .RPT file from when the bug occurred, If on a private dedicated server, please attach the server's .RPT from when the bug occurred.
